observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: a. Municipal Mayor (i) instruct the Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical count of all its property, plant and equipment to determine its physical existence and condition and submit to the Audit Team the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment and Report on the Physical Count of Local Road Network, duly reconciled with the accounting and property records; (ii) direct the GSO/Acting Municipal Treasurer to initiate the proper disposal of all unserviceable properties, complete the information on the IIRUP for submission to the Audit Team; and maintain the property cards for all PPE; b. Municipal Engineer conduct an inspection on all ongoing projects to determine its status then prepare the report for submission to the Municipal Accountant; and c. Municipal Accountant (i) determine the unidentified PPE for proper valuation and allocation of its cost against its estimated useful life; (ii) consider data cleansing of the PPE SLs taking into consideration the new threshold in the recognition of PPE of 50K in accordance with COA Circular 2024-006 dated March 14, 2024; (iii) upon submission of the Status Report of the Municipal Engineer, recognize and adjust the PPE in the books; (iv) determine, reclassify and provide for the revised estimated useful life for the PPE items acquired at lump-sum prices based on the cost breakdown or relative fair value; (v) reclassify road opening and clearing of access road to their appropriate PPE accounts; and (vi) provide complete disclosure in the NTFS for the road network system, including length, value, reductions, and other movements during the year.
observations.managementAction: The Management implemented actions but failed to create an Inventory Committee for the conduct of the regular physical count; the Acting Municipal Treasurer/GSO Designate still failed to initiate proper disposal proceedings of unserviceable properties; the Municipal Engineer has yet to conduct the inspection of the projects upon submission of the list by the Municipal Accountant. The Municipal Accountant has yet to determine or validate the balances of the PPE items due to the absence of proper turnover of records from the previous Municipal Accountant. The Municipal Accountant will adjust and reconstruct the PPE records upon completion of verifications and trainings.
observations.reason: The Action Plan submitted indicates that although an Inventory Committee was created by Executive Order for CY2025, the physical count of PPE has not yet been conducted and the corresponding RPCPPE and RPCLRN have yet to be submitted. Construction-in-progress adjustments and transfers of PPE from TF to GF were not completed due to non-submission of Certificates of Completion and lack of proper turnover of records.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: a. Municipal Accountant (i) immediately submit to the Audit Team the lacking bank statements for the 12 depository accounts; (ii) exert diligent effort to reconcile the balances per General Ledgers (GLs) and Subsidiary Ledgers (SLs), and to identify the unreconciled balances in various bank accounts aggregating ₱67,892,885.57, otherwise, ensure that the discrepancies and unreconciled balances would not accumulate in the ensuing years; (iii) prepare the revised BRS for all accounts with book balances duly reconciled with the GLs and SLs and accordingly record the reconciling items that need adjustment in the books including the adjustment of cash in bank and payables balances resulting from stale checks; (iv) in coordination with the Acting Municipal Treasurer, obtain from the bank the required documentation like credit/debit advices as bases in making the necessary adjustments in the books; and (v) submit copies of the bank statements, disbursement vouchers (DVs) and the related supporting documents of the unrecorded check disbursements reported in the BRS; b. Acting Municipal Treasurer (i) provide the Audit Team a proof that the DVs, ObRs and supporting documents of the unrecorded disbursements were already submitted to the Accounting Office, otherwise exhaust all means to locate and secure the missing transaction documents and immediately submit to the Municipal Accountant to facilitate their recording in the books of accounts; and (ii) account for all unclaimed checks which had been outstanding for over six (6) months from date of issue wherein the original DVs and supporting documents are still in the custody of the treasury office and cancel these stale checks; and c. Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant observe strictly the rules and regulations on the reporting and accounting for stale checks provided in Section 83(b)(4) of the LTOM.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant submitted the bank statements for the 12 depository accounts to the Audit Team and started reconciliation; however, unreconciled balances increased in CY 2024. The Municipal Accountant will request copies of previous years' bank statements from the bank and obtain supporting documentation from the Acting Municipal Treasurer. The Acting Municipal Treasurer will account for unclaimed checks and cancel stale checks. The Municipal Accountant will submit required documentation when available.
observations.reason: Reconciliation remained ongoing; unadjusted reconciling items and unreconciled beginning balances persisted. The Municipal Accountant indicated inability to complete reconciliation due to lack of available copies of prior years' bank statements and missing supporting documents from the Treasury.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: a. LCE and Sangguniang Bayan strictly comply with the pertinent provisions of RA No. 7160 regarding the instrument required for authority in effecting changes in the Annual Budget; and b. Municipal Accountant, Acting Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Budget Officer, and other members of the Local Finance Committee immediately submit LBP Form No. 8 or the Statement of Funding Sources, and LBP Form No. 9 or the Statement of Supplemental Appropriation for all supplemental budgets; and provide the Audit Team the detailed computation/schedules and related documents supporting the declared sources of funds for the budget changes totaling ₱59,006,628.47; submit the documents showing the object of expenditures of newly appropriated PPAs in the resolutions; and ensure that for the ensuing years declaration of sources of fund for supplemental budgets are properly supported with valid basis and in accordance with the provisions of Sections 321 and 336 of R.A. No. 7160.
observations.managementAction: Various budget changes in CY 2024 were still not covered with appropriation ordinances. The Municipal Budget Officer submitted LBP Form No. 9 for Ordinance No. 2024-001 covering savings from various PPAs amounting to ₱8,243,076.05; however LBP Form No. 8 was not signed by the Municipal Accountant. The Municipal Budget Officer will submit LBP Form Nos. 8 and 9 to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: Various budget changes in CY 2024 were still not covered by appropriation ordinances; LBP Form No. 8 was not submitted or properly signed; full supporting documentation for declared sources of funds was not provided at time of audit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: a. Municipal Accountant submit the revised SCBAA and SAAOB for CY 2023 and submit the required documents; b. Municipal Budget Officer reconstruct the RAAOs for CY 2023 and henceforth maintain the required registries per responsibility center to monitor appropriations, allotments and obligations; and c. Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant coordinate to reconcile records and submit the required reconciliations.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Budget Officer maintained the current RAAOs for CY 2024 and will reconcile records with the Municipal Accountant. The Municipal Accountant to submit revised documents when completed.
observations.reason: The Municipal Budget Officer maintained RAAO for current appropriations but RAAOs and SAAOB for continuing appropriations were not provided; required reconciliations and submissions were not completed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: a. Municipal Accountant (a) reconcile the Net Cash Flow from Operating Activities to Surplus/Deficit for CY 2023 and disclose the reconciliations in the Notes to Financial Statements (NTFS) pursuant to Paragraph 29 of IPSAS 2; (b) Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer immediately submit the reconciliation on Actual Amounts presented in the SCBAA and in the Statement of Financial Performance for CY 2023 and henceforth reconcile their records on allotments and obligations monthly, verify resulting discrepancies and submit the required reconciliation statement within the prescribed period; and c. Municipal Engineer submit a complete report on the costs, description and specifications and cost segregation of all road components existing in the Municipality regardless of its year of implementation to the Municipal Accountant as bases for disclosures in the NTFS.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant provided the disclosure in the NTFS for CY 2024. The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to reconcile records on allotments and obligations; the Municipal Engineer was requested to provide complete report but details provided were incomplete and were not aligned with accounting schedules. Municipal Accountant to follow up.
observations.reason: No full reconciliation was made between budget and accounting records; Municipal Engineer's report on road components was incomplete and not aligned with schedules; reconciling documentation pending.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated the prior year recommendations that the Municipal Mayor, through the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer: a. strictly demand the O&E and the DOs to submit liquidation of their cash advances with complete supporting documents at the end of the year and require them to refund immediately any unexpended balance as prescribed by COA Circular No. 97-002; b. regularly serve written notices to all concerned AOs to demand settlement of their outstanding cash advances, copy furnish the Audit Team for monitoring; c. consider withholding the salaries of AOs still connected with the Municipality who refuse to settle their CAs without justifiable reason; d. implement a sound internal control over the granting, liquidation and monitoring of cash advances; e. exert efforts in determining the causes of negative cash advance balances and unreconciled balances and thereafter effect the necessary adjustment in the books of accounts; f. request for the write-off of all the dormant accounts duly supported with the necessary documents pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005; g. hire and designate another bonded Disbursing Officer for special or regular cash advances; h. discontinue the practice of granting additional cash advances to O&E with outstanding cash advance/s and closely monitor the granting and liquidation of their cash advances; i. discontinue the practice of payment of procurement through cash advances and reimbursements of travelling expenses of O&E; and j. require the Treasurer/DO to promptly submit the required documents to the Municipal Accountant for the timely recording of cash advances and its liquidations.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant issued written notices to concerned AOs to demand settlement of outstanding cash advances; the Municipality stopped the practice of payment of procurement through cash advances and of reimbursements of travelling expenses through cash advances. The Municipality hired and designated an additional Disbursing Officer. Partial reconstruction of cashbook records is ongoing; no full write-off requests yet. Some beginning balance unreconciled amounts persist.
observations.reason: Partial actions implemented (written notices, ceased certain practices, additional DO designated) but lapses remained: many AOs still had unsettled advances; reconstruction of cashbooks incomplete; dormant accounts not yet written off; negative/unreconciled balances remain.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant (a) reconstruct and thereafter maintain separate subsidiary ledgers for each fund transfer and submit the same to the Audit Team for audit guidance and reference; and (b) support the quarterly trial balances with subsidiary schedule showing in detail the individual balances of the fund transfer.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant reconstructed some SLs and submitted the trust agreement to the Audit Team. The Municipality returned the unutilized balance of one completed project to the source agency. Further reconciliation ongoing.
observations.reason: Reconstructed SLs, especially for program/project, were not fully submitted to the Audit Team for validation; reconciliation and complete schedules remain outstanding.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant exert effort in reconstructing the Subsidiary Ledgers of the Cash Local Treasury account detailing the cash accountability of the previous and current Municipal Treasurers in accordance with Sections 111.1 and 114.2 of PD No. 1445 and submit the same to the Audit Team for validation; and b. Acting Municipal Treasurer regularly reconcile her cashbooks with available accounting records in accordance with Section 63(F) of the LTOM.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant reconstructed some SLs and submitted to the Audit Team. Acting Municipal Treasurer is reconstructing cashbooks; some partial reconciliations done.
observations.reason: Reconstruction of SLs is incomplete; Acting Municipal Treasurer failed to maintain complete and proper cashbooks hindering full reconciliation; discrepancies remain.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant (i) conduct an analysis of the Accounts Payable account and effect the necessary correcting entries to reclassify the payable items to its appropriate liability accounts; (ii) provide a complete details of the payable accounts as at yearend; (iii) verify the validity of dormant and unsubstantiated payables and confirm the existence of actual claimants; (iv) process the payment of valid claims, otherwise, initiate reversion of unconfirmed payables to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund; (v) record all obligations per Disbursement/Payroll Vouchers; and (vi) maintain the Index of Payments to monitor subsequent payments to creditors.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant provided the schedule of Accounts Payable as at December 31, 2024. Partial actions taken; investigation and verification ongoing.
observations.reason: Municipal Accountant has yet to conduct full verification and analysis of Accounts Payable; complete details of payable accounts were not provided; dormant payables and unconfirmed items still under review.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor give priority for the revision, updating and consolidation of the Revised Revenue Code of Linapacan, Palawan 2014 pursuant to Section 191 of the LGC of 1991 by issuing an Executive Order for the creation of the Oversight Committee in accordance with Item 6.4 of DILG-DOF JMC No. 2019-01 to maximize the revenue generating capacity of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The revision of the Revenue Code was scheduled in July 2025.
observations.reason: Revision, updating and consolidation of the Revised Revenue Code was still ongoing and scheduled for July 2025.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor through the BAC Secretariat (a) consolidate the PPMP of the end-users into an indicative APP in a prescribed form with complete details and furnish the Audit Team of the said report; (b) process procurement based on the approved APP; and (c) attach BAC Resolution for all purchases indicating the recommended mode of procurement.
observations.managementAction: The BAC Secretariat required the end-users to submit their PPMPs and consolidated the procurement of CSE of all offices in CY 2024. However, some disbursement vouchers in CY 2024 were still not supported with BAC Resolution indicating the mode of procurement.
observations.reason: End-users submitted PPMPs but the consolidated APP was not submitted to the Audit Team; some purchases/disbursements lacked attached BAC Resolution indicating the mode of procurement.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant immediately reconcile the records on Due to GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth to determine the cause/s of the negative/unremitted and dormant balances to facilitate remittance thereof and accordingly made the necessary adjustments in the books; and b. Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer ensure that funds collected by the municipal government in behalf of other government agencies are remitted fully and within the prescribed due dates to avoid incurrence of interest and penalties.
observations.managementAction: Reconciliation of accounting records for GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth started; Municipality resorted to online remittance for withheld funds; some withheld taxes remained unremitted at year-end.
observations.reason: Reconciliation ongoing; there were still unremitted withheld employee contributions as at year-end; full remittance not completed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management (a) convene the Local Solid Waste Management Board for the formulation and execution of the Solid Waste Management Plan (SWMP) to immediately address the noted deficiencies in audit; (b) enforce segregation at source of solid wastes; and (c) secure Environmental Compliance Certificate of the dumpsite.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality is currently developing a new dumpsite but construction of access road is ongoing which delayed operations; SWMP revision underway.
observations.reason: No approved 10-year SWMP; new dumpsite operationalization delayed due to access road construction; ECC application not yet made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: a. Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer ensure reconciling records on DRRMF and present the correct reconciled amounts in the NTFS, SCBAA and SAAOB and in the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of the DRRM Fund; b. Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer cause the immediate transfer of the unexpended DRRMF to the LDRRMF STF bank account; c. Municipal Accountant immediately (i) trace, locate and recover the documents related to the unknown balance of Trust Liabilities-DRRMF and reconstruct the SLs of the unexpended DRRMF in the STF by year of transfer; (ii) record the necessary adjustments in the books; and (iii) upon approval of the LDRRM Council, record the reversion of the unexpended LDRRMF balances which had remained unutilized within the allowable five years under the STF account back to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund; and d. Municipal Accountant, Municipal Budget Officer, and MDRRM Officer stop the practice of charging ineligible expenses to the DRRMF.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer transferred some unexpended DRRMF amounts to STF bank account; Municipal Accountant to trace unknown balances and reconstruct SLs; some transfers of prior years made while others pending. MDRRM Officer stopped charging ineligible expenses starting July 2024.
observations.reason: Some transfers made but prior year balances of DRRMF remained untransferred and unrecorded; unknown balance of ₱100,000.00 unresolved and not reverted; SLs reconstruction ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated prior year recommendations that (a) OIC-MPDC in coordination with the Municipal Engineer (i) conduct a feasibility or preliminary engineering study before project proposal to assess the viability of identified development projects; and (ii) review the unimplemented PPAs and the balances of continuing appropriations at year-end to determine possible re-programming and discontinuance of the projects; b. Municipal Engineer expedite and strictly monitor the implementation of ongoing priority projects while evaluating the performance of contractors involved; and c. Municipal Mayor through the MPDC and the Municipal Budget Officer (i) stop the practice of charging ineligible expenses to the 20% DF and henceforth ensure that the utilization of the 20% DF is in accordance with the provisions of DILG-DOF-DBM JMC No. 1; and (ii) consider financing the operations of the solid waste management out of its share from the collections of environmental fee in accordance with Section 8, Chapter III of Ordinance No. 2016-087.
observations.managementAction: The MPDC and Municipal Engineer to conduct feasibility studies; the Report on Government PPAs as at 3rd quarter 2025 submitted showing some projects ongoing and some reprogrammed; Management stopped charging Solid Waste Management operations to the 20% DF starting July 2024.
observations.reason: Several projects remained unimplemented as at audit; savings from completed projects remained unprogrammed; full feasibility studies and reprogramming actions ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: a. Municipal Accountant immediately reconcile the records on Due to BIR to determine the cause/s of the negative/unremitted balances to facilitate remittance thereof and accordingly made the necessary adjustments in the books; and b. Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer ensure that funds collected by the municipal government in behalf of other government agencies are remitted fully and within the prescribed due dates to avoid incurrence of penalties.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant started reconciliation of accounting records; Municipality resorted to online remittance of withheld taxes; transition ongoing.
observations.reason: Reconciliation ongoing; there were still unremitted withheld taxes at year-end; transition to online remittance still on-going.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer, trace the accounting records to determine the cause/s of the discrepancies between the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts of all funds and record the necessary adjustments in the books to present the correct balances in the financial statements; and b. review and analyze the CY 2022 transactions and prepare appropriate adjustments and henceforth review the correctness of accounting entries in the JEVs and Journals, and provide cross-reference for each transaction in the Subsidiary Ledger to facilitate tracing to source documents and for monitoring purposes.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant started reconciliation of reciprocal accounts from CY 2020 to 2024 with the 20% DF and GF - Proper. Identified entries partially adjusted in the books in 2024. Reconciliation for other remaining funds ongoing.
observations.reason: Reconciliation ongoing; identified entries partially adjusted in 2024; remaining balances pending reconciliation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: a. Municipal Mayor, in coordination with the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer, exhaust all possible means to enforce the collection of dormant receivables by sending demand letters to the debtors, otherwise, request for write-off of dormant receivables; and b. Municipal Accountant (i) strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2016-005 on the required analysis of dormant accounts by performing the regular and periodic verification, analysis and validation of the existence of the receivables and determine the concerned debtors, accountable officers for strict monitoring and disposition of said accounts; and (ii) provide allowance for impairment of dormant receivables as required under IPSAS 29 for the fair presentation of receivables and equity account in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: No full implementation yet. The Municipal Accountant has no available record on the dormant accounts thus could not perform regular verification, analysis and validation. Demand letters were issued; some actions ongoing.
observations.reason: Allowance for impairment not yet provided; Municipal Accountant lacks complete records on dormant receivables; collection/enforcement actions ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the BAC Secretariat immediately submit to the Audit Team the lacking documents to facilitate technical evaluation and validation of the projects; strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-001 and 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 to establish the legality, validity and propriety of the disbursements; effect necessary adjustments to correct erroneous recording of release of retention money amounting to ₱49,534.31; and Municipal Treasurer to submit proof of payments for final billing and release of retention money for the Road Concreting and ensure DVs are properly certified and approved before issuance of checks.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant checked the regularity and completeness of supporting documents prescribed under Section 9.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 before processing claims for payments and ensured compliance with 2016 Revised IRR; adjustments in the books made where applicable. The Municipal Treasurer to submit proof of payments. Some disbursement vouchers for CY 2024 remain unsupported and pending validation; retention adjustments being effected.
observations.reason: Required supporting documents and DVs for final payments and release of retention money were not fully submitted to the Audit Team; adjustments pending and validation ongoing.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Mayor (i) create an Inventory Committee, who will conduct a complete physical count of all its PPE to determine its existence and condition and submit to the Audit Team the RPCPPE and RPCLRN, duly reconciled with the accounting and property records; and (ii) direct the GSO/Acting Municipal Treasurer to initiate the proper disposal of all unserviceable properties, complete the information on the IIRUP for submission to the Audit Team; and maintain the property cards for all PPE;
b. Municipal Engineer conduct an inspection on all ongoing projects to determine its status then prepare the report for submission to the Municipal Accountant; and
c. Municipal Accountant (i) determine the unidentified PPE for proper valuation and allocation of its cost against its estimated useful life; (ii) consider data cleansing of the PPE SLs taking into consideration the new threshold in the recognition of PPE of 50K in accordance with COA Circular 2024-006 dated March 14, 2024; (iii) upon submission of the Status Report of the Municipal Engineer, recognize and adjust the PPE in the books; (iv) determine, reclassify and provide for the revised estimated useful life for the PPE items acquired at lump-sum prices based on the cost breakdown or relative fair value; (v) reclassify road opening and clearing of access road to their appropriate PPE accounts; and (vi) provide complete disclosure in the NTFS for the road network system, including length, value, reductions, and other movements during the year.

We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Accountant (i) exert diligent effort to reconcile the balances per GLs and SLs, and to identify the unreconciled balances in various bank accounts aggregating ₱75,694,327.75, otherwise, ensure that the discrepancies and unreconciled balances would not accumulate in the ensuing years; (ii) prepare the revised BRS for all accounts with book balances duly reconciled with the GLs and SLs and accordingly record the reconciling items that need adjustment in the books including the adjustment of cash in bank and payables balances resulting from stale checks; (iii) in coordination with the Acting Municipal Treasurer, obtain from the bank the required documentation like credit/debit advices as bases in making the necessary adjustments in the books; and (iv) submit copies of the bank statements, disbursement vouchers (DVs) and the related supporting documents of the unrecorded check disbursements reported in the BRS;
b. Acting Municipal Treasurer (i) provide the Audit Team a proof that the DVs, ObRs and supporting documents of the unrecorded disbursements were already submitted to the Accounting Office, otherwise exhaust all means to locate and secure the missing transaction documents and immediately submit to the Municipal Accountant to facilitate their recording in the books of accounts; and (ii) account for all unclaimed checks which had been outstanding for over six (6) months from date of issue wherein the original DVs and supporting documents are still in the custody of the treasury office and cancel these stale checks; and
c. Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant observe strictly the rules and regulations on the reporting and accounting for stale checks provided in Section 83(b)(4) of the LTOM.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant exert effort in reconstructing the SLs of the Cash Local Treasury account detailing the cash accountability of the previous and current Municipal Treasurers in accordance with Sections 111.1 and 114.2 of PD No. 1445 and submit the same to the Audit Team for validation.

We recommended that the:
a. Acting Municipal Treasurer strictly observe the maintenance of complete and updated cashbooks, provided with sufficient information to facilitate its regular reconciliation with accounting records in accordance with the provisions of Section 63(F) of the LTOM; and
b. Acting Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant reconcile their records and take-up the necessary adjusting entries in their respective records and submit to the Audit Team a reconciliation statement thereon.

We recommended that the:
a. Acting Municipal Treasurer (i) submit to the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of every year a duly certified list of taxpayers showing the amount due and collectible for the year based on updated RPT Account Registers or Taxpayer’s index card as basis for the setting up of RPT and SET Receivables; and (ii) provide the Municipal Accountant with a certified list of all real property tax delinquencies as at year-end as basis in making necessary adjustments in the books; and
b. Municipal Accountant henceforth, (i) set up the RPT and SET Receivables and Deferred RPT and SET accounts at the beginning of the year based on the Acting Municipal Treasurer’s duly certified list of taxpayers showing the amount due and collectible for the current year as well the uncollected taxes in prior years; and (ii) upon receipt of the list of all real property tax delinquencies as at year-end, review pertinent records and prepare adjustments in the books to reflect the correct balances of RPT and SET Receivables and other related accounts as at year-end.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant trace the accounting records to determine the cause/s of the discrepancies between the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts of all funds and record the necessary adjustments in the books to present the correct balances in the financial statements.

We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Budget Officer reconstruct the RAAOs and SAAOB for CY 2024 considering the sources of funds for the budget changes made during the year, as well as the continuing appropriations, and submit it to the Audit Team; and
b. Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer immediately submit the reconciliation on Actual Amounts presented in the SCBAA and in the Statement of Financial Performance for CY 2024 and henceforth reconcile their records on allotments and obligations monthly, verify resulting discrepancies and submit the required reconciliation statement within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant immediately reconstruct the lacking subsidiary ledgers (SLs) and schedules of the above accounts depending on the availability of reliable records and submit to the Audit Team pursuant to Sections 111.1. and 114.2. of P.D. No. 1445 to substantiate the account balances reported in the financial statements as at year end.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant reconstructed the SLs as at January 1, 2021.
observations.reason: The reconstructed SLs as at January 1, 2021 were not submitted to the Audit Team for validation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant transfer the PPE items under TF books with aggregate cost of ₱14,314,161.11 to the GF books and provide depreciation and provide adjustments for the correct Accumulated Depreciation of the PPE in the books in accordance with PAG 3 of IPSAS 17 for the fair presentation of PPE Accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant transferred the PPE items under TF books with aggregate cost of ₱14,314,161.11 to the GF books.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant could not provide depreciation due to the absence of breakdown and details of the PPE items transferred from the TF; accumulated depreciation details still to be provided.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: a. Municipal Accountant (i) review the pertinent provisions of the Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) pertaining to the unutilized balance of the completed project and communicate with the source agency for the immediate return thereof, if warranted; (ii) in coordination with the Municipal Engineer, immediately submit the lacking documents supporting the payments relative to the Construction of National Child Development Center (NCDC); and (iii) through the Municipal Treasurer, return the unexpended balance of the LGU Counterpart for the Construction of NCDC and effect the necessary entries in the books; and b. Municipal Health Officer and HRM Officer Designate provide the lacking documents in the grant of OCA/HEA in CYs 2021 and 2022 to the Municipal Accountant for submission to the Audit Team and henceforth ensure strict compliance with the roles and responsibilities of their offices.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality returned the unutilized balance of the completed project to the source agency. The Municipal Health Officer and HRM Officer Designate submitted the lacking documents in the grant files to the Municipal Accountant who provided grant documents to the Audit Team. Municipal Accountant will coordinate with Municipal Engineer to submit technical documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor (i) designate/appoint a personnel preferably under the Office of the Municipal Treasurer/General Services Office, who will be in-charge with the requisition, monitoring and reporting of the FOL; (ii) in coordination with the Municipal Accountant, implement a sound internal control and guidelines on FOL and submit that guidelines to the Audit Team for adoption and strict implementation of the guidelines on fuel consumption of government vehicles of the Municipality; and (iii) submit the required documentation of the FOL consumption for CY 2023 in accordance with COA Circular No. 77-61 to establish its validity, reasonableness and regularity.
observations.managementAction: The Mayor designated a GSO Designate and issued Memorandum No. 95-2024 adopting and strictly implementing guidelines on fuel consumption. The Municipal Accountant to submit required documentation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: a. OIC-GSO designate/Acting Municipal Treasurer conduct a comprehensive physical inventory of all municipal properties and submit the detailed inventory thereof to the audit team to support and establish the existence, condition, and proper valuation of all real properties; b. Municipal Accountant exert efforts to determine the details of the balance in the Land account based on the physical count and thereafter maintain real property ledger cards with complete details/information for each real property; and c. Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the OIC-GSO designate/Acting Municipal Treasurer, urgently obtain and maintain Certificates of Title and Tax Declarations for all municipal properties. Immediate steps should be taken to register the properties under the Torrens Title System to comply with Section 148 of COA Circular No. 92-386 and safeguard municipal assets from risks such as third-party claims and unlawful occupancy.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer/GSO Designate conducted an inventory of machineries, furniture and fixtures and office equipment. The Municipal Accountant obtained copies of available documents from the Municipal Assessor. The Acting Municipal Treasurer/GSO Designate to assist in obtaining titles and tax declarations.
observations.reason: The land documents obtained from the Municipal Assessor were still subject for verification by the Municipal Accountant; full set of Certificates of Title and Tax Declarations not yet obtained; steps to register under Torrens Title pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: a. Sangguniang Bayan revisit the provisions of Ordinance No. 2016-087 to establish a clear sharing scheme of the Environmental Fee otherwise amend or supplement the IRR of the Ordinance, if any, and submit the report to the Audit Team; b. Municipal Accountant (i) setup a special account in the General Fund for the Environmental Fee; (ii) submit a collection and utilization reports including its supporting documents to verify the validity of the charges since the enactment of the Code; c. All Punong Barangays (i) come up with a feasible sharing scheme of the Environmental Fee and propose the same to the Sangguniang Bayan; and (ii) based on the report of the Municipal Accountant, submit a proposal related to the environment and tourism project chargeable to the 10 percent share of the 80 percent gross collections; and d. Acting Municipal Treasurer (i) determine the total collections from the enactment of the ordinance and reconcile the same with the report from the Municipal Tourism Office; (ii) transfer the 20 percent share of the concerned barangays based on the approved new sharing scheme; and (iii) transfer the 15 percent share of the General Fund to the GF-Proper bank account; and e. Municipal Mayor as chairperson of both the Municipal Tourism Council and Environmental Management Board revisit the guidelines, if any, else formulate a clear-cut policy on the implementation of the Code taking into consideration the noted deficiencies in audit subject for review of the Sangguniang Bayan.
observations.managementAction: The Sangguniang Bayan adopted Resolution No. 2024-148 dated August 5, 2024 conveying the collective and unified stand of the SB clarifying the intent of Chapter III, Section 8 of Municipal Ordinance No. 2016-087 stating no 20% share of the EF collections was due to the barangays since collection was performed by the designated EF collectors of the LGU since 2016. The Sangguniang Bayan adopted Ordinance No. 2024-211 (The Revised Tourism Code of 2024) on December 16, 2024. The Municipal Accountant set up a special account in the General Fund in CY 2024 with beginning balances for reconciliation. Acting Municipal Treasurer determined total collections from enactment was ongoing.
observations.reason: Sangguniang Bayan passed resolutions and ordinance clarifying intent; Municipal Accountant set up special GF account; reconciliation and remittance to barangays or transfer of shares pending and ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: a. Sangguniang Bayan (i) conduct a legislative inquiry on the collection, utilization and reporting of the fund to determine the accountabilities of the parties involved; (ii) revisit the existing plans, policies and procedures, and pass a resolution for the adoption of mitigating measures to correct the identified deficiencies/errors in audit; and (iii) amend Ordinance Nos. 2019-023 and 2019-024 taking into consideration the noted deficiencies; b. Municipal Accountant implement a sound internal control over collection, utilization and reporting of the fund; c. Acting Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant implement special accounts in the General and Trust Funds of the municipality for the environmental fees of MPA; d. MPA Management Body of Barangay Calibangbangan and Barangonan (i) submit the report of collections and utilization of the Fund since the enactment of the ordinance to the Audit Team; and (ii) submit a written explanation for the non-demarcation of buoys on the MPAs; and e. Punong Barangay of Barangonan submit a written explanation for the non-remittance of collected MPA fees to MGL; and f. Municipal Mayor and Punong Barangay of Barangonan submit the related documents and reports pertaining to the MPA of Barangay Barangonan.
observations.managementAction: The Sangguniang Bayan adopted Resolution No. 2024-148 dated August 5, 2024 and Ordinance No. 2024-211 (The Revised Tourism Code of 2024) on December 16, 2024. The Municipal Accountant implemented Special Accounts in the General and Trust Funds for environmental fees. MPA Management Body submitted documents pertaining to the MPA of Barangay Barangonan to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor through the Municipal Accountant or authorized representative promptly submit to the Auditor the transmittal letter and the supporting documents pertaining to the cash advances granted for confidential fund in a manner prescribed in Section 6.1.8.4 and 6.2.3.6 of COA-DBM-DILG-GCG-DND Joint Circular No. 2015-01.
observations.managementAction: The SDO submitted the transmittal letters and supporting documents pertaining to the cash advances for confidential funds.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: a. formulate a coordinated process/procedures on requisition, delivery, inspection and issuance of supplies and equipment; and b. review and identify the controls to be implemented taking into consideration the audit mentioned lapses and implement internal control procedures as laid down in Chapter 8 of the Handbook on PSMS; and require the Municipal Accountant upon receipt of the Disbursement Vouchers for audit review to reject/return the incompletely filled out forms attached thereto to compel the concerned accountable officers' compliance with the handbook on PSMS.
observations.managementAction: The Management implemented centralized numbering of the forms and required end-users to comply with Handbook on PSMS. Municipal Accountant to reject incomplete DVs attached to incompletely filled forms.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor (a) require the General Services Officer Designate and the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to comply with the provision of the 2016 RIRR of R.A. No. 9184 concerning procurement of CSE; and (b) instruct the BAC to consolidate all CSE purchase requests of all departments as shown in the Annual Procurement Plan and utilize the functionality of the mPhilGEPS in ordering CSE through the Virtual Store.
observations.managementAction: The BAC consolidated the procurement of CSE of all offices in CY 2024 and utilized DBM-PS Depot operations; a Certificate of Non-Availability from DBM-PS was obtained and submitted for items not available in depot.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor through the Sangguniang Bayan create the GSO position, if warranted, and hire a qualified person, otherwise revert the GSO duties and functions to the Municipal Treasurer in accordance with Section 490(b)(viii) of the LGC.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality issued Executive Order No. 78 on October 10, 2024 reassigning GSO functions to the Acting Municipal Treasurer. The Municipality is to create the GSO position if warranted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Acting Municipal Treasurer (i) ensure that all collections of the Collecting Officer (CO) assigned at the Municipal Treasurer’s Office are remitted intact and regularly at the close of each business day pursuant to Section 90(A) of the LTOM, 2nd edition; (ii) require all Accountable Officers to maintain and regularly update their respective cashbooks, prepare their respective Report of Collections and Deposits (RCDs), and Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms (RAAF), all in the format prescribed by the LTOM; (iii) henceforth prepare an RCD for all collections received, including remittances from COs; (iv) strictly observe the maintenance of complete and updated cashbooks; and b. Municipal Mayor immediately facilitate the application for fidelity bond of the two (2) designated COs for the collection of environmental fee, and henceforth ensure that all COs are properly bonded in accordance with law to safeguard the municipal government’s funds.
observations.managementAction: The COs applied for fidelity bonds; the BAC consolidated CSE procurements; Acting Municipal Treasurer required COs to remit daily collections and prepared RCDs; some COs still had incomplete cashbooks; Municipal Treasurer to ensure bonding and strict compliance.
observations.reason: Some COs still failed to fully remit and maintain complete cashbooks for CY 2024; fidelity bond applications in process.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: a. LDRRM Council (i) include in the LDRRMFIP the list of PPAs to be charged against the prior years' unexpended LDRRMF; (ii) ensure that PPAs included in the LDRRMFIP are included in the LDRRMP; and (iii) assess unexpended fund aged more than five years for possible reversion; b. Municipal Mayor, Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer ensure that the prior years' unexpended LDRRMF are released and utilized for new expenditure items only upon submission of a revised investment plan duly approved by the sanggunian upon recommendation of the LDRRMC in accordance with the approved LDRRM Plan; and c. Municipal Accountant, Municipal Budget Officer and MDRRM Officer stop the practice of charging ineligible expenses to the DRRMF and ensure that utilization is in accordance with law.
observations.managementAction: The LDRRMC revised LDRRMFIP for CY 2024 and included some PPAs; Municipality returned some unutilized balances and stopped charging ineligible expenses starting July 2024. Municipal Accountant to transfer remaining unexpended funds and reconstruct SLs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain a separate special account in the General Fund for the 20% Development Fund as provided under Section 313 of RA No. 7160 and Chapter 6 of MNGAS, Volume I for LGUs and subsequently submit the related financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant maintained a special account in the General Fund for the 20% DF and submitted its financial statements for CY 2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant (a) immediately submit to the Audit Team the duly authenticated copies of the MOA/Trust Agreement with Barangay Maroyogroyog pertaining to the fund transfer and other documentary requirements; and (b) make the necessary adjustments to correct the entries made for the fund transferred to the Barangay by recognizing 'Due from LGUs'.
observations.managementAction: The trust agreement was forwarded to the Audit Team and the Municipal Accountant adjusted the entries made for the fund transferred to the Barangay by recognizing 'Due from LGUs'.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the (a) ICO-Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant reconcile their reports and records to determine the cause/s of the cash deficit in the TF books so that remedial measures can be implemented; and (b) the Municipal Accountant to explain the incurrence of cash deficit, and henceforth ensure the completeness and accuracy of records to enhance the integrity of accounting reports.
observations.reason: As per the Audit Team’s calculation, the Municipality still incurred a deficit of ₱20,860,567.56 as of December 31, 2021, computed by deducting the current liabilities from the total Cash and Cash Equivalents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: LCE, through the Municipal Accountant, serve written notices to all concerned accountable officers requiring settlement of the outstanding cash advances and institute legal action against them, if warranted; Municipal Accountant reconstruct the subsidiary ledgers for Advances to SDOs and Due from Officers and Employees accounts as of December 31, 2020 and immediately submit copies thereof to the Audit Team for verification; prepare the Schedule/Report of Unliquidated Cash Advances (RUCA) as of December 31, 2020 and ensure that the totals thereof are duly reconciled with the balances reported in the General Ledger, Trial Balance and Statement of Financial Position; immediately submit to the Audit Team the RUCA as of December 31, 2020 containing complete details as to name of the AOS, position/designation, purpose of cash advance, date of grant, unliquidated amount, due date of liquidation, age of the cash advances and status of employment of the AOs whether they are still connected with the municipal government, retired, resigned, dead or can no longer be traced; LCE and the Municipal Accountant, in all instances, ensure that each provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 are strictly observed and that all cash advances are fully liquidated at the end of the year.
observations.reason: The Audit Team was not furnished of any demand letters issued by the former Municipal Mayor had not issued demand letters to erring accountable officers. Moreover, the same deficiency was still observed in the audit of CY 2021 accounts hence, warranting reiteration of the audit observation in Finding No. 3 of this AAR. Due to the inadequacy of records, the Municipal Accountant was not able to reconstruct the subsidiary ledgers for the Advances to SDOs and Due from Officers and Employees accounts. Moreover, the same deficiency was still observed in the audit of CY 2021 accounts hence, warranting reiteration of the audit observation in Finding No. 3 of this AAR. Due to the inadequacy of records, the Municipal Accountant was not able to prepare the Schedule/Report of Unliquidated Cash Advances (RUCA) as of December 31, 2020. Moreover, the same deficiency was still observed in the audit of CY 2021 accounts hence, warranting reiteration of the audit observation in Finding No. 3 of this AAR. The Audit Team has not yet received the Schedule/Report of Unliquidated Cash Advances (RUCA) as of December 31, 2020. Moreover, the same deficiency was still observed in the audit of CY 2021 accounts hence, warranting reiteration of the audit observation in Finding No. 3 of this AAR. The Audit Team still noted material balances of unliquidated cash advances hence, reiterated in Finding No. 3 of this AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: Inventory Committee (i) immediately conduct the physical count of all municipal property; (ii) prepare the Report on the Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) and the Report on the Physical Count of the Local Road Network (RPCLRN) to be reconciled with the Property Cards, PPE Ledger Cards (PPELC), Local Road Network Ledger Cards (LRNLC) and other accounting records; and (iii) submit a copy thereof to the Audit Team for verification and audit; Municipal Accountant (i) maintain PPELC for each class of PPE, Real Property Ledger Card for each real property and LRNLC for local road networks and ensure that all subsidiary ledger cards contain complete details/description of the property to facilitate proper reconciliation with the RPCPPE and RPCLRN; (ii) provide depreciation for depreciable PPE with aggregate cost of ₱61,902,379.62 in accordance with Paragraphs 43 and 71 of IPSAS 17 and submit to the Audit Team the updated lapsing schedule for depreciation expenses of all depreciable PPE; and (iii) transfer to the General Fund the cost of completed projects booked as PPE under the Trust Fund totaling ₱14,314,161.11; Municipal Treasurer, as the acting GSO, (i) maintain complete property cards with complete information; and (ii) maintain a local road inventory and road map and Local Road Network Property Card pursuant to COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015.
observations.reason: The Management had not submitted a complete physical inventory report of the Municipal Government’s PPE as of December 31, 2020 warranting reiteration of the audit observation in Finding No. 5 of this AAR. The PPELC, RPLC and LRNLC which are the SLs of the Municipal Government’s PPE accounts are not yet maintained hence, reiterated in Finding No. 5 of this AAR. Depreciation was still not provided for PPE accounts with aggregate cost of ₱67,830,657.88 hence, warranting reiteration of the audit observation in Finding No. 6 of this AAR. The complete set of property cards are not being maintained by the Treasury Office hence, warranting reiteration of the audit observation in Finding No. 5 of this AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant trace, locate and recover the documents related to the above accounts and record the details obtained in the respective subsidiary ledgers. Henceforth, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant exert efforts to maintain the required books of accounts including subsidiary ledgers pursuant to Section 114 (2) of P.D. No. 1445 and submit the required subsidiary schedules of GL balances and subsidiary ledgers to substantiate the account balances reported in the financial statements.
observations.reason: The deficiency was still observed in the audit of the CY 2021 accounts hence, warranting reiteration of the audit observation in Finding No. 10 of this AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit the required reconciliations and disclosures on the Notes to Financial Statements for CY 2020 regarding the Actual Amounts presented in the SCBAA and in the Statement of Financial Performance; total Road Network System; and the information pertaining to DRRM Fund. These notes are integral part of the Financial Statements, hence the Municipal Accountant should ensure that all reconciling items have basis and supported with schedules and necessary details.
observations.reason: The required disclosures were not submitted by the Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant identify the cause of the variance totaling ₱526,215.80 between the amounts reported in the NTFS and the amounts per trial balances and make the necessary adjustments, duly supported with adequate documentation.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant did not apprise the Audit Team on the cause/s of the numerical discrepancies.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the MSWDO (i) submit the accomplished SAC Forms per beneficiary and Memorandum of Agreement between the LGU and the DSWD in order to facilitate complete evaluation of the transaction; and (ii) see that all disbursement vouchers are properly supported with complete documentation before making any payment to ensure proper liquidation of cash advances for SAP.
observations.reason: The Audit Team is yet to receive copies of the accomplished SAC forms.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: Municipal Budget Officer immediately submit to the Audit Team the Supplemental Budget or Appropriation Ordinance covering the realignment of the 20% DF to COVID-related PPAs; MPDC immediately submit to the Audit Team copy/ies of Supplemental Annual Investment Programs detailing the COVID-related PPAs funded out of the 20% DF; Municipal Accountant (a) strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular 97-002 in processing grants of cash advances; (b) ensure that liquidations are properly accounted for in the books in accordance with applicable provisions; (c) check/review the completeness and propriety of supporting documents before processing and certifying the claims for payment as part of their internal audit service; and (d) see that disbursements are made in compliance to existing laws, rules and regulations.
observations.reason: The Audit Team was not furnished with a Supplemental Budget covering the realignment of the 20% DF to COVID-related PPAs. The Audit Team was not furnished with Supplemental Investment Programs. Infractions of COA Circular No. 97-002 were still observed in the audit of CY 2021 accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: Municipal Engineer and the MPDC immediately submit to the Audit Team the consolidated status report/information of all the infrastructure projects started, completed and still on-going as of December 31, 2020; and LCE, Municipal Engineer and MPDC strictly comply with the guidelines of COA Circular No. 2013-004 regarding information and publicity of PPAs as well as the general provisions of succeeding GAAs on transparency in the status of implementation of government PPAs.
observations.reason: The Audit Team is yet to receive the consolidated status report/information of all the infrastructure projects started, completed and still on-going as of December 31, 2020. COA Circular No. 2013-004 was still not observed as the Audit Team was not furnished with quarterly reports of on-going, completed and to be implemented PPAs.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:

     1) Municipal Mayor issue a written demand to the former  ICO-Municipal
        Treasurer for the  immediate  turnover  of  his  cashbooks  to  the
        Municipal Accountant to determine his total accountability;

     2) Municipal Accountant:

        a. prepare and submit to the Audit  Team  the  bank  reconciliation
           statements and the subsidiary ledgers for the  seven  depository
           accounts and henceforth observe regular preparation of  BRS  and
           proper maintenance of SLs for  all  bank  accounts  pursuant  to
           Section 3 of COA Circular No. 96-011;

        b. prepare the necessary journal  entries  to  take  up  the  valid
           reconciling items consisting of unrecorded deposits,  unrecorded
           fund transfers and unrecorded disbursements, duly supported with
           pertinent documents;

        c. reverse the journal entries made in recording the  “unreconciled
           balances from previous years” totaling ₱2,936,199.00 as the same
           were not supported with adequate documentation; and

        d. in coordination with the Acting  Municipal  Treasurer,  properly
           identify the unreconciled  balances  in  various  bank  accounts
           aggregating  ₱28,670,097.59.   Once   these   items   are   duly
           identified, record the same in the books  and  ensure  that  the
           journal entries to record the same are supported  with  complete
           and valid documentation;

     3) Acting Municipal Treasurer:

        a. ensure proper maintenance of cashbooks for Cash in Treasury  and
           Cash in Bank pursuant to Sections 26 and 27 of the  NGAS  Manual
           for LGUs, Volume II;

        b. ensure that all collections are deposited intact  in  accordance
           with Section 28 of COA Circular No. 92-382, whenever there is an
           opportunity to transact with the bank; and

        c. properly report the LGU’s collections and deposits by  providing
           complete information in the Report of Collections  and  Deposits
           to allow matching and monitoring of the same.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant (a) exercise  caution  in
recording financial transactions to forestall frequent erroneous  accounting
entries as they could materially affect the accuracy and  fair  presentation
of account balances in the financial statements; (b) ensure the accuracy  of
the accrual of RPT and SET Receivables at the beginning  of  the  year;  and
(c) determine the cause of the abnormal balances of the SET Receivables  and
the Deferred SET income account and make necessary  adjustments  to  correct
the abnormal balances duly supported with adequate documentation.

We reiterated the prior year’s audit recommendations that the:

     a. Municipal Mayor, through the Municipal  Accountant,  serve  written
        notices  to  all  concerned  accountable  officers  demanding   the
        immediate settlement of their cash advances; and
     b. Municipal Accountant (i)  prepare  the  subsidiary  ledgers  as  of
        December 31, 2021 of all Accountable Officers and submit  the  same
        to the Audit Team for  evaluation;  (ii)  prepare  and  submit  the
        Report of Unliquidated Cash Advances (RUCA) as of December 31, 2021
        and  henceforth  observe  its  quarterly  submission  pursuant  to
        provisions of COA Circular No. 2016-005.

We recommended that the:


     a. Municipal Mayor, in coordination with the Municipal Accountant  and
        the Municipal Treasurer, exhaust all possible means to enforce  the
        collection of dormant receivables by sending demand letters to  the
        debtors, otherwise, request for write-off of  dormant  receivables;
        and


     b. Municipal Accountant (i) strictly comply with the provisions of COA
        Circular No. 2016-005 on the required analysis of dormant  accounts
        by performing the regular and periodic verification,  analysis  and
        validation of the existence of the receivables  and  determine  the
        concerned debtors, accountable officers for strict  monitoring  and
        disposition of said accounts; (ii) prepare the quarterly  aging  of
        dormant receivables, unliquidated cash advances and submit them  to
        the Audit Team for audit guidance and reference; (iii) provide
        allowance for impairment of dormant receivables as  required  under
        IPSAS 29 for  the  fair  presentation  of  receivables  and  equity
        account in the financial statements.

We recommended that the:


     a. Inventory Committee (i) immediately conduct the physical  count  of
        all municipal property; (ii) prepare the  Report  on  the  Physical
        Count of PPE and the Report on the Physical Count of the Local Road
        Network to be reconciled with the Property Cards, PPE Ledger Cards,
        Local Road Network Ledger Cards and other accounting  records;  and
        (iii) submit a copy thereof to the Audit Team for verification  and
        audit;


     b. Municipal Accountant (i) maintain PPELC for each class of PPE, Real
        Property Ledger Card for each real property  and  LRNLC  for  local
        road networks and ensure that all subsidiary ledger  cards  contain
        complete details/description of the property to  facilitate  proper
        reconciliation with the RPCPPE and RPCLRN;  and  (ii)  ensure  full
        disclosure of the total road network system  in the NTFS;


     c. Municipal  Engineer  provide  the  Municipal  Accountant  with  the
        complete description and cost segregation of  road  components  for
        road projects; and


     d. Acting Municipal Treasurer, in her capacity  as  property  officer,
        (i) maintain complete property cards with complete information; and
        (ii) maintain a local road inventory and road map  and  Local  Road
        Network Property Card pursuant to COA Circular No.  2015-008  dated
        November 23, 2015.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant provide depreciation  for
depreciable PPE with aggregate cost of  ₱67,830,657.88  in  accordance  with
Paragraphs 43, 59 and 71 of IPSAS 17 for a fairer  presentation  of  account
balances in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant  (i)  record  only  valid
claims against government funds for assets already  delivered  and  received
by the Municipality and expenses incurred in the current accounting  period;
(ii) perform a thorough review of the accounting entries made  for  the  set
up of Accounts Payable in the GF and SEF books  totaling  ₱8,112,763.07  and
if found to be lacking of sufficient documentary requirements,  prepare  the
necessary adjusting entries by debiting the Accounts Payable  and  crediting
Prior Period Adjustment for the expenses and reversal of accounting  entries
for the assets; and (iii)  be  mindful  of  her  duty  to  ensure  that  all
transactions recorded in  the  books  are  duly  supported  with  valid  and
adequate documentation.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant (a) maintain separate SLs
for each fund transfer and submit the same  to  the  Audit  Team  for  audit
guidance and reference; and (b) support the quarterly  trial  balances  with
subsidiary schedule showing in detail the individual balances  of  the  fund
transfers.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant (a) immediately submit to
the Audit Team the duly authenticated  copies  of  the  MOA/Trust  Agreement
with Barangay  Maroyogroyog  pertaining  to  the  fund  transfer  and  other
documentary requirements prescribed under Item 3.1.1  of  COA  Circular  No.
2012-001; and (b) make the necessary  adjustments  to  correct  the  entries
made for the fund transferred to Barangay Maroyogroyog by  recognizing  “Due
from LGUs”.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant  trace,
locate and recover the documents related to the above  accounts  and  record
the details obtained in the respective subsidiary ledgers.


      Henceforth, we recommended that the Municipal  Accountant  exert  more
efforts to maintain the required  books  of  accounts  including  subsidiary
ledgers pursuant to Section 114  (2)  of  P.D.  No.  1445  and  provide  the
necessary subsidiary schedules of GL  balances  and  subsidiary  ledgers  to
support the account balances presented in the financial statements.

We recommended that the:

     a. Municipal Accountant and the BAC Secretariat (i) immediately submit
        to the Audit Team the lacking  documents  to  facilitate  technical
        evaluation and validation of the  projects;  and  (ii)  henceforth,
        strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-001 on
        complete documentation and RA No. 9184 and its Revised IRR  on  the
        procurement and implementation of infrastructure projects;


     b. Municipal Accountant (i) check the regularity and  completeness  of
        supporting documents prescribed under Section 9.1 of  COA  Circular
        No. 2012-001 before  processing  claims  for  payments  and  ensure
        compliance with 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184  to  establish  the
        legality, validity and propriety of  the  disbursements;  and (ii)
        effect the necessary adjustments to correct the erroneous recording
        of the release of retention money amounting to ₱49,534.31; and


     c. Municipal Treasurer (i) submit the proof of payments for the  final
        billing and release of retention money for the Road  Concreting  at
        Sitio Balo-Balo, Barangay San Miguel  totaling  ₱2,684,510.87;  and
        (ii)  ensure  that  disbursement   vouchers   bear   the   required
        certification and approval of the proper officials  before  issuing
        checks for payments.

We recommended that the:


     a. Municipal Accountant  and  the  Municipal  Treasurer  transfer  the
        unexpended balance of the LDRRMF to the Special Trust Fund pursuant
        to Section 5.1.10 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 to ensure that funds
        are available solely for disaster  risk  reduction  and  management
        activities;


     b. Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer (a) reconcile
        their records on allotments and obligations monthly and verify  the
        resulting discrepancies; and (b) exercise  due  care  in  preparing
        their  respective  reports  to  prevent  inaccurate  or  misleading
        information;


     c. Municipal Accountant (i) maintain subsidiary ledgers for  transfers
        of  unutilized DRRMF to the special trust fund by year of transfer;
        (ii) make  the  necessary  adjustments  to  correct  the  erroneous
        recording  of  Medical  Equipment  totaling  ₱182,000.00  to  other
        accounts; and (iii) provide adequate disclosure on  the  LDRRMF  in
        the NTFS using the prescribed format in Annex D of COA Circular No.
        2012-002; and


     d. Municipal Accountant, through the LDRRM Officer submit the  Monthly
        Report on Sources and  Utilization  of  the  DRRMF  duly  certified
        correct  by  the  Municipal  Accountant  in  accordance  with   the
        prescribed deadline stated in Section 5.1.1  of  COA  Circular  No.
        2012-002.

We recommended that the Acting  Municipal  Treasurer  (i)  immediately
deposit the remaining SET collections for CYs 2020 and 2021 to the SEF  bank
account; (ii) ensure the preparation of separate reports of collections  for
RPT and SET and automatically deposit the proceeds of the additional 1%  RPT
accruing to the SEF depository account pursuant to Sections  82  and  86  of
the MNGAS for  LGUs,  Volume  I;  and  (iii)  henceforth,  report  all  bank
deposits in the RCD in accordance with Section 32, Volume I and Section  49,
Volume II  of  the  NGAS  Manual  in  order  to  check  proper  matching  of
collections and deposits.

We recommended that the: Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant dutifully observe and implement the provisions of Section 36 of COA Circular No. 92-382 and the existing laws and regulations on the prompt remittances of amounts due to the GSIS, PhilHealth and Pag-IBIG to prevent imposition of charges and penalties and to ensure continued availment of benefits of the employees; and (b) submit an explanation on the non-remittance of the balance of the Due to PhilHealth account under the TF books of ₱508,436.32 which had remained non-moving for two years; Municipal Accountant determine the specific transactions which caused the negative year-end balance of the Due to PhilHealth account and immediately effect the necessary corrections supported by sufficient documentation; and LCE, as the agency head, see that the officials who are responsible for the late remittances to the PhilHealth are made personally liable for the surcharges incurred amounting to ₱5,000.92.
observations.managementAction: Mandatory contributions were regularly remitted in CY 2021

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: Local Chief Executive (LCE), as agency head: (a) be mindful of the various provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 on the grant and liquidation of cash advances and the existing government procurement regulations (e.g., RA. financial statements in compliance with Section 3 of COA Circular No. 96-011;
exercise caution in analysing financial transactions, closely monitor and
strengthen review of JEVs, CDJ/GJs and GLs to ensure that all financial
transactions are properly and accurately captured and taken up in the
books to prevent the occurrence of accounting errors and negative account
balances as they could materially affect the accuracy and fair presentation
of account balances in the financial statements; and
review the previous accounting entries affecting the cash accounts to
identify the causes of negative CLT account balance and effect the
necessary correcting entries duly supported with sufficient documentation
including a satisfactory explanation therefor.

compliance with Section 69 of P.D. No. 1445, Section 2 of COA Circular No. 97-002
dated February 10, 1997 and Section 32 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I, to
forestall possible misuse of collections; and (b) submit a written explanation for
failure to deposit collections intact regularly.

3. Municipal Accountant:
a. be mindful and cautious in seeing that all financial transactions and operations of the Municipality are recorded in conformity with accounting standards and in accordance with laws, rules and regulations;
b. carefully review all accounting entries before approving JEVs and recording transactions in the special and general journals and ensure that all financial transactions are duly covered by JEVs;
c. check the propriety and completeness of supporting documents before claims are processed for payment and prepare the Accountant’s Advice only for those transactions covered with DVs supported with documents in accordance with Section 344 of R.A. No. 7160.
observations.managementAction: Management did not submit reason/s for non-implementation of the audit recommendation and the AAPSI.

observations.managementAction: Management did not submit any reason for non-implementation of the audit recommendation and the AAPSI.

No. 1445, Section 2 of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 and Section 32 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I.

No. 1445.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: a. LCE create an Inventory Committee which shall (i) conduct physical inventory of all municipal property; (ii) prepare the Report on the Physical Count of PPE and Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System to be reconciled with the Property Cards, PPE Ledger Cards, Road Network Ledger Cards and other accounting records; and (iii) submit a copy thereof to the Audit Team; b. Municipal Accountant: (i) maintain complete and updated GLs for all PPE accounts for all funds and provide the necessary details such as source documents which were the bases in recording thereof; (ii) prepare complete lapsing schedules for all depreciable assets and ensure monthly depreciation is recognized in the books; (iii) re-evaluate and re-compute total accumulated depreciation of all depreciable PPE and make necessary adjustments; (iv) maintain PPELC for each class of PPE account, RPLC for each real property and LRNLC for road networks and ensure all ledger cards contain complete details; (v) secure documents/certificate of completion from Municipal Engineer; (vi) submit proof of ownership or rights over land properties; (vii) record all PPE transactions in accordance with Chart of Accounts prescribed by COA; and (viii) ensure DVs covering payment for purchase of property and equipment are supported with duly signed/acknowledged ARE; c. Municipal Treasurer maintain complete property cards; and d. ensure transfers of completed projects booked under TF to GF as appropriate and provide depreciation.
observations.managementAction: In its written reply, the Management commented that the Inventory Committee is committed to make improvements regarding the control and management of PPE starting CY 2020. Physical inventory count of all municipal properties will be conducted and the required inventory reports shall be prepared and thereafter to be reconciled with the accounting records. As part of the management’s initial step towards implementing the audit recommendations, all municipal department heads were asked to provide a listing of PPE under their accountabilities. The listings that will be provided will then be reconciled with the existing GSO and accounting records. In addition, the Accounting Office will also start maintaining PPE ledger cards. Completed PPE under the TF books will also be transferred to the GF books.
observations.reason: The deficiency was still observed in the audit of CY 2018 accounts, thus reiterated in Part II of this report.

We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare and maintain complete GLs and SLs for all funds; submit the schedule of GL balances for all real accounts; post all entries not part of special journals to the General Journal; submit Working Trial Balances for all funds; prepare JEV with complete required details; prepare CDJ, CRJ, CkDJ and GJ per month for all funds; and provide JEV and complete supporting documents for all bank debit and credit memos.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant submitted the year-end financial statements for CY 2018 on August 23, 2020. In response to AOM No. L2020-001 (2019) dated February 17, 2020 sent to the Audit Team’s official e-mail address on February 27, 2020, the Municipal Accountant acknowledged her failure to meet the deadline for the submission of the financial reports and transaction documents. She remarked that having been appointed as Municipal Accountant only on October 16, 2019, she went through an adjustment phase in her present position.
observations.reason: The deficiency was still observed in the audit of CY 2018 accounts, thus reiterated in Part II of this report.
