observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:
1. Municipal Mayor ensure that the provisions of Sections 344 and 347 of
R.A. No. 7160 on certification/approval of DVs and rendition of
accounts; Sections 4.5 and 4.6 of P.D. No. 1445 on disbursement of funds
and complete documentation; and Section 7.2.1(a) of COA Circular No.
2009-006 on submission of reports and supporting documents to the
Auditor are strictly adhered in all transactions of the Municipality.
2. Municipal Accountant:
a. prepare and maintain GLs and SLs for all cash accounts and
immediately submit to the Audit Team the required subsidiary
schedule of GL balances of cash accounts for all funds and SLs per
depository bank;
b. dutifully observe the prompt preparation and submission of monthly
Bank Reconciliation Statements to the Audit Team, including the
bank statements and valid bank debit/credit advices, for verification
purposes to ascertain the correctness of the Cash in Bank account
balance in compliance with Section 3 of COA Circular No. 96-011;
c. exercise caution in analysing financial transactions, closely monitor
and strengthen review of JEVs, CkDJ/CDJ/GJ and GLs to ensure
that all financial transactions are properly and accurately captured
and taken up in the books to prevent the frequent occurrence of
accounting errors and inaccurate and misleading information as they
would materially affect the accuracy and fair presentation of account
balances in the financial statements;
d. review the previous accounting entries affecting the cash accounts to
identify the causes of negative CLT account balance and effect the
necessary correcting entries duly supported with sufficient
documentation including a satisfactory explanation therefor; and
e. submit to the Audit Team the lacking documentary requirements for
disbursements totalling ₱9,749,622.74 such as copies bank
statements and paid checks.

We recommended that the ICO-Municipal Treasurer deposit all collections
promptly and intact with the authorized government depository bank in
compliance with Section 69 of P.D. No. 1445, Section 2 of COA Circular No. 97-
002 dated February 10, 1997 and Section 32 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs,
Volume I.

We recommended that the:
1. ICO-Municipal Treasurer update the Real Property Tax Account
Registers or Taxpayer’s Index Cards, prepare and submit to the
Municipal Accountant the certified list of taxpayers showing the
current/prior years amount due and collectible at the beginning of every
year as basis in setting up the RPT and SET Receivables; and
2. Municipal Accountant (a) ensure the proper setting up of RPT/SET
Receivables and their corresponding reciprocal accounts at the beginning
of the year based on the Treasury Office’s duly certified list of real
property taxpayers and the amounts due and collectible for the year; (b)
reconcile the recorded RPT and SET Receivable and the Deferred RPT
Income and Deferred SET Income accounts with the Treasury report on
the unpaid taxes for the current year as well the uncollected taxes in prior
years and thereafter update the said accounts balances.

We recommended that the:
1. LCE, through the Municipal Accountant, serve written notices to all
concerned accountable officers requiring settlement of their outstanding
cash advances and institute legal action against them, if warranted;
2. Municipal Accountant:
a. reconstruct the subsidiary ledgers for Advances to SDOs and Due
from Officers and Employees accounts as of December 31, 2018 and
immediately submit copies thereof to the Audit Team for verification;
b. prepare the Schedule/Report of Unliquidated Cash Advances (RUCA)
as of December 31, 2018 and ensure that the totals thereof are duly
reconciled with the balances reported in the General Ledger, Trial
Balance and Statement of Financial Position; and
c. immediately submit to the Audit Team the RUCA as of December 31,
2018 containing complete details as to name of the AO,
position/designation, purpose of cash advance, date of grant,
unliquidated amount, due date of liquidation, age of the cash
advances and status of employment of the AOs whether they are still
connected with the municipal government, retired, resigned, dead or
can no longer be traced.
3. LCE and the Municipal Accountant, in all instances, ensure that each
provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 are strictly observed and that all
cash advances are fully liquidated at the end of the year.

We recommended that the:
1. Inventory Committee (i) immediately conduct the physical inventory of
all municipal property; (ii) prepare the Report on the Physical Count of
PPE and the Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System
to be reconciled with the Property Cards, PPE Ledger Cards, Road
Network Ledger Cards and other accounting records; and (iii) submit a
copy thereof to the Audit Team;
2. Municipal Accountant (i) maintain PPELC for each class of PPE, RPLC
for each real property and LRNLC for local road networks and ensure
that all ledger cards contain complete details/description of the property
to facilitate proper reconciliation with the RPCPPE and RPCLRN; (ii)
submit the updated lapsing schedule for depreciation expenses of all
depreciable PPE; (iii) provide depreciation for depreciable PPE with
aggregate cost of ₱52,173,096.34 in accordance with Paragraphs 43 and
71 of IPSAS 17; and (iv) transfer to the General Fund the cost of
completed projects booked as PPE under the Trust Fund totaling
₱5,975,402.63; and
3. Municipal Treasurer, as the acting GSO, (i) maintain complete property
cards with complete information; and (ii) maintain a local road inventory
and road map and Local Road Network Property Card pursuant to COA
Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile the reciprocal
accounts Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds, determine the
cause/s of disparity in their balances and effect the necessary adjustments in
the books to present their correct balances in the financial statements.
Henceforth, review the correctness of accounting entries in the JEVs to ensure
the accuracy of account balances in the financial statements.

We recommended that the:
1. Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to immediately
submit to the Audit Team the duly authenticated copies of the
MOA/Trust Agreement with the PGP pertaining to the fund transfer and
other documentary requirements prescribed under COA Circular No.
2012-001;
2. Municipal Accountant (a) make the necessary adjustments to correct the
entries made for the fund transferred to PGP by recognizing “Due from
LGUs”; and (b) maintain SL therefor to ensure proper monitoring of its
utilization including the implementation of the project; and
3. Municipal Budget Officer (a) submit the Obligation Request, Monthly
SAAOB for CY 2018, complete supplemental budgets, realignments,
augmentations and/or any authorized changes in the Annual Budget for
CY 2018; and (b) maintain and update RAAO for all budgetary
expenditure.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal
Treasurer ensure timely and full remittance of all taxes withheld by the Municipal
Government to the BIR to avoid penalties and legal charges.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant trace, locate and recover
the documents related to the above accounts and record the details and other
information obtained in the respective subsidiary ledgers to support the general
ledger account balances.
Henceforth, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure that the
Municipal Accountant maintains the required books of accounts including
subsidiary ledgers pursuant to Section 114(2) of P.D. No. 1445 and submits the
required subsidiary schedules of GL balances and subsidiary ledgers to
substantiate the account balances reported in the financial statements pursuant
Sections 70 and 72 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor (a) compel the Municipal
Accountant to submit the complete financial statements, monthly financial
reports, journals, subsidiary schedules of general ledger accounts for all funds
and other required reports in such form and within the period prescribed in the
NGAS Manual for LGUs and COA Circular No. 2010-001 dated March 2, 2010;
and (b) ensure that appropriate actions are taken on the deficiencies noted in
audit and in disciplining subordinates who are the subject of repeated audit
findings consistent with the responsibilities of the agency head stated in
Section 7.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant (i) exercise utmost
diligence in the preparation of financial reports in order to produce reports that
are reliable, usable and accurately reflect the financial position, performance and
cashflows of the LGU at any reporting period; and (ii) ensure thorough review of
journal entry vouchers, special journals, reports and the financial statements
before submission for audit, thus minimizing or possibly eliminating the
occurrence of errors in presentation and mathematical computation.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit the required
reconciliations and disclosures on the Notes to Financial Statements for CY 2018
regarding Net Cash Flow from Operating Activities; Actual Amounts presented in
the SCBAA and in the Statement of Financial Performance; total Road Network
System; and the information pertaining to DRRM including the details of the
unexpended balance of LDRRMF. These notes are integral part of the Financial
Statements, hence the Municipal Accountant should ensure that all reconciling
items have basis and supported with schedules and necessary details; and (b)
strictly comply with the accounting and reporting guidelines for the DRRMF of
LGUs under COA Circular No. 2014-002.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (i) direct the Department
Heads concerned to immediately prepare a consolidated report on all
management actions taken relative to the audit observations and
recommendations included in the Annual Audit Report for CY 2017 using the
prescribed AAPSI Form; and (ii) henceforth, strictly adhere to the prescribed
period for the submission of the AAPSI as provided under the GAA.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the ICO-Municipal Treasurer should apply strict implementation of controls over collections and remittances to avoid misappropriation and/or concealment of irregularities in handling of cash.
a. Collectors did not turn-over their collections promptly and prepare the related Report of Collections and Deposits (RCDs), resulting to late remittances to the ICO-Municipal Treasurer and delayed recording by the ICO-Municipal Accountant, contrary to Sections 29 and 35 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) for Local Government Units (LGUs), Volume I and Section 26 of COA Circular No. 92-382 dated July 3, 1992.
Collectors/tellers should religiously observe daily remittance of their collections, together with the issued official receipts, and preparation and submission of the corresponding RCDs to the treasurer or cashier to whom the cash collected should be turned over.
We recommended that the ICO-Municipal Treasurer should check the accuracy of RCDs submitted by the collectors and check the Statement of Accountable Forms by undertaking physical count of all accountable forms remaining in the custody of the collectors and verifying both used and unused forms to ascertain that all collections are actually remitted. Collectors and the ICO-Municipal Treasurer should also properly fill up and sign the reports in compliance with Section 30 of the said manual.
We recommended that the ICO-Municipal Treasurer should prepare the RCD consolidating the remitted collections and consisting all the deposits made by him, supported with the RCDs of collectors, official receipts and validated deposit slips in compliance with Section 35 of the said manual.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require all AOs (ICO-Municipal Treasurer, collectors and disbursing officers) who are handling cash or being granted cash advances to maintain cashbooks and strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997. All AOs should (a) properly maintain their cashbook by fund; (b) regularly update, balance and reconcile records with the cash on hand daily or as necessary; and (c) reconcile the cashbooks with the records of the ICO-Municipal Accountant at least quarterly to ensure that the recorded transactions are complete, reliable, accurate and timely.
He should also enjoin the former ICO-Municipal Treasurer and the ICO-Municipal Accountant to reconcile their books of accounts and assign competent personnel to participate in the reconciliation process, exert effort in tracing up origin of those questionable amounts, identify those accountable persons responsible for such deviations and assist in providing pertinent documents to support necessary adjustments in the books to reflect the correct balances of the account in each fund.
Lastly, all AOs should be reoriented of their duties and responsibilities, the related internal controls and policies being implemented.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following:
We reiterate that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) should create an Inventory Committee, consisting of a representative from the Office of the Municipal Mayor, Treasurer’s Office and Accounting Office.
Require each department head to fast track inventory of all properties under their office and submit the inventory report to the committee for validation and verification of the correctness and existence of the property.
The inventory committee should check the condition of the property, any damage, obsolete and unserviceable property shall be reported in the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable and Waste Material Report and immediately request for disposal from the Commission.
Compel the inventory committee to fully accomplish the RPCPPE, indicating the actual count and complete details, and the results of the actual count shall be reconciled with the property and accounting records. All discrepancies between physical and book inventories must be investigated, adjusted and reconciled immediately.  From thereon, perform periodic inventory of PPE and prepare report on the results thereof, in accordance with existing regulations.
The LCE should require the Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain ledger cards for each class and category of  property and equipment.  Work back on the previous years, based on the delivery receipt, acceptance and inspection report and other source documents and prepare property ledger card indicating acquisition date, cost and the brief descriptions which serves as the subsidiary ledgers of fixed assets per classs and category.  Based on ledger cards prepare lapsing schedule for the computation and monitoring of depreciation charges.
The Treasurer Office to maintain Property Cards per class and category of property, plant and equipment indicating acquisition date, the brief descriptions and work back on the previous years and reconcile with the records of the accounting office to determine the correctness of the property records.  Update the property issue slip/PAR to check custodianship of the property.
Management Comment:
During the audit exit conference, Management commited to implement the audit recommendations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor cause the strict adherence to laws, rules and regulations pertaining to the submission of financial statements, records and reports to the Commission on Audit from the ICO – Municipal Treasurer, OIC – Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer in order that all financial reports and transaction documents are submitted on time.

During the audit exit conference, the Municipal Accountant committed to adhere strictly to the audit recommendations.

We recommended the following:
The Local Chief Executive should require all units/divisions of the Municipality to meticulously and judiciously prepare their individual Annual Procurement Plans (APP) and respective Project Procurement Management Plans (PPMP) for their different programs, activities and projects (PAPs) and submit the same to the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) for consolidation and identification of the appropriate mode of procurement and to the Budget Officer for evaluation and inclusion in the budget proposal.
Adopt public bidding as the general mode of procurement in compliance with Section 10 of R.A. 9184. Resort only to shopping or canvass on highly exceptional cases or when conditions warrants.
Instruct the ICO – Municipal Treasurer to stop the practice of paying goods and materials out of cash advances and strictly adhere to the requirement that all payments must be made by check and directly paid to the suppliers, contractors, dealers and creditors.  Withdrawals or cash advance to disbursing officer should be limited only for payment of salaries and wages and other personnel services and other petty operating expenses.  Stop the practice of procuring materials on a reimbursement basis.
Perform thorough review and evaluation of the legality of the alternative mode of procurement before resorting to shopping and small value procurement;
Stop the practice of splitting of purchases to circumvent requirements of existing terms and regulations;
The Municipal Mayor, the BAC must ensure that Detailed Engineering activities has been conducted before any procurement of Infrastructure Projects.
Instruct the ICO-Municipal Accountant to compute and withhold taxes in all government purchases including those on reimbursement basis; and
Strictly adhere with the provisions of R.A. No. 9184 and its revised IRR and GPPB Resolution No. 09-2009 dated November 23, 2009 in all government procurement.

We recommended that the management should transfer the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF to a special trust fund pursuant to Section 21 of R.A. No. 10121 to ensure that funds are available for disaster risk reduction and management activities of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council and maintain a separate bank account. Prepare and submit the LDRRMFIP in the format prescribed in COA Circular 2012-002. Further, the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of the DRRMF duly certified correct by the Municipal Accountant should be submitted to this Office on or before the 15th day after the end of each month. Maintain separate subsidiary ledgers for LDRRMF and disclose the details of the unexpended balance in the Notes to the Financial Statements in the Year-End financial reports. Lastly, the Municipality should be judicious and prudent in the utilization of the LDRRMF, taking into consideration the existing rules and regulations governing the utilization of the said fund.

We recommended strict adherence to PCW-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01. The Municipal Mayor and the GAD Focal Person should ensure that GAD Fund allocation shall be utilized for PPAs that directly address gender disparities and promote gender equality, gender-responsive governance, women’s economic empowerment and protect and fulfill women’s human rights. The total disbursement for identified PPAs should be equivalent to the required fund allocation for GAD.
Submit the Annual GAD Accomplishment Report in the prescribed format and within the deadline so that timely review and evaluation of the same is facilitated.
Stop the practice of utilizing GAD Fund for financial assistance.
